design_philosophy.core_loop: Select punches (jab, cross, hook, uppercut), block opponent attacks, evade in two directions, repeat until knockout or decision victory
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Reading opponent patterns and timing defensive maneuvers while managing stamina across three-round matches
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Assume the role of George Foreman at age 43 pursuing heavyweight championship by uniting title belts across three fictional boxing circuits
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game is deliberately constrained in player movement and available moves, prioritizing pattern recognition and defensive timing over offensive variety—creates friction that reviewers interpreted as poor design rather than intentional simplicity

difficulty.checkpointing: Password system used in career mode to save progress between matches (no battery backup)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; reviewers criticized 'little opportunity to dodge opponent's punches' and 'limited movement from main character,' suggesting difficulty stems from restricted player options rather than scalable challenge settings

progression.unlock_mechanism: Career mode progression through sequential match victories against opponents in three boxing circuits; password system gates advancement
replayability_hooks: Career mode completion with multiple circuits to conquer

world_building_techniques: Licensed boxer (George Foreman) provides celebrity draw
Career structure implies fictional rival circuits (three title belts to unite)
Progressive damage visualization on boxer portraits (becoming 'battered and bloody')

faction_indicators: Three distinct boxing circuits representing different power structures in heavyweight division
danger_signals: George Foreman's age (43) signals underdog narrative—questioning legitimacy of comeback
Unified belt quest implies conflict between competing sanctioning bodies
environmental_storytelling: Boxer portrait damage accumulation shows physical toll of championship quest
Ring setting implicitly conveys broadcast spectacle and institutional stakes

sources_used: Wikipedia: George Foreman's KO Boxing (Gameplay, Versions, Reception sections)
